name: Temperature Test
description: Measures the temperature of the wine or must to verify it is within the target range for the current operation.

name: Brix Test
description: Measures the sugar content (degrees Brix) of must or juice using a refractometer or hydrometer.

name: Gravity Test
description: Measures specific gravity to track fermentation progress.

name: pH Test
description: Measures the pH of wine or must to assess acidity and microbial stability.

name: Dissolution Test
description: Evaluates clarity and sediment after dissolution — used for fining and filtration assessments.

name: ABV Test
description: Measures the alcohol by volume (ABV) of wine using an ebulliometer or distillation method, with optional temperature correction.

name: Residual Sugar Test
description: Determines residual sugar concentration (g/L) in finished wine using final gravity readings.

name: Sensory Evaluation
description: Organoleptic assessment of wine appearance, aroma, and taste by trained tasters.

name: Cold Stability Test
description: Tests wine for tartrate crystal formation under cold temperatures to determine cold-stabilisation need.

name: Microbial Stability Test
description: Incubation test to detect microbial activity — turbidity, film, gas, or colour change — before bottling.
